tasks.title: Add health stat to all unit types
tasks.description: Add maxHealth to the UNIT_TYPES registry (Elon=100, Rocktimus=50), propagate health and maxHealth through createUnit(), and display health in the sidebar unit info panel.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
1. UNIT_TYPES has maxHealth field: elon=100, rocktimus=50.
2. createUnit() returns objects with both health and maxHealth set from the type definition.
3. The #unit-info panel shows 'Health: X/Y' for the selected unit.
4. Health values initialize correctly for all unit instances.
5. No combat or damage mechanics added.
6. Pattern is clear for future unit types to follow.
tasks.claudeMd:
# Project Context You are adding a health stat to a vanilla HTML/CSS/JS browser game. There is NO build system, NO package manager, NO framework. The entire game engine lives in `game.js` (~1400 lines). UI is in `index.html`, styles in `style.css`. ## Conventions (MUST follow) - Use `var` declarations (the codebase uses `var` everywhere, not `let`/`const` — except for top-level constants which use `const`) - DOM manipulation via `document.getElementById()` - No semicolons are omitted — always use semicolons - Match existing indentation: 4 spaces inside the IIFE, 8 spaces inside functions - Unit properties follow the pattern: `movesLeft`/`movesMax` for mutable/max pairs ## Files to Change ### 1. `game.js` — UNIT_TYPES registry (lines 35–52) Add `maxHealth: 100` to `elon` and `maxHealth: 50` to `rocktimus` entries. Place it as the last property in each object. ### 2. `game.js` — createUnit() function (lines 54–65) Add two properties to the returned object: ```js health: def.maxHealth, maxHealth: def.maxHealth, ``` Place them after `turnsRemaining`. ### 3. `index.html` — unit info panel (after line 24, the #unit-moves div) Add between #unit-moves and #unit-lifespan: ```html
Health: 100 / 100
``` ### 4. `game.js` — updateUI() function (around line 765) After the moves-max update, add: ```js document.getElementById("health-current").textContent = unit.health; document.getElementById("health-max").textContent = unit.maxHealth; ``` ### 5. `style.css` — health display styling Add after the `#unit-moves` rule (around line 116): ```css #unit-health { color: #cc4444; font-size: 0.85rem; margin-top: 2px; } ``` This matches the pattern of `#unit-moves` and `#unit-lifespan` styles. ## Gotchas - The entire game.js is wrapped in an IIFE `(function() { ... })()`. All edits are inside this scope. - `UNIT_TYPES` uses `const` but the unit objects returned by `createUnit` are plain mutable objects. - The `updateUI()` function runs on every state change — make sure to always update both health spans. - Do NOT add combat/damage mechanics. This is data plumbing only. - Do NOT modify `.github/workflows/` — pushes touching that path will be rejected.

checklist.description: Add maxHealth field to elon entry in UNIT_TYPES
steps: Open game.js and locate the UNIT_TYPES object (line 35)
Add `maxHealth: 100` as the last property of the elon entry
Verify the comma-separated syntax is correct

checklist.description: Add maxHealth field to rocktimus entry in UNIT_TYPES
steps: Locate the rocktimus entry in UNIT_TYPES (line 44)
Add `maxHealth: 50` as the last property
Verify the comma-separated syntax is correct

checklist.description: Propagate health and maxHealth in createUnit()
steps: Locate createUnit() function (line 54)
Add `health: def.maxHealth,` and `maxHealth: def.maxHealth,` to the returned object after turnsRemaining
Verify the object literal syntax is correct

checklist.description: Add health display HTML to unit info panel
steps: Open index.html and locate the #unit-info panel
Add a #unit-health div between #unit-moves and #unit-lifespan with spans for health-current and health-max
Verify the HTML is well-formed

checklist.description: Update health display in updateUI() function
steps: Locate updateUI() in game.js (around line 760)
After the moves-max update line, add lines to set health-current and health-max textContent from unit.health and unit.maxHealth
Verify the DOM IDs match the HTML
Open style.css and locate the #unit-moves rule (around line 112)
Add a #unit-health rule after #unit-lifespan with color: #cc4444, font-size: 0.85rem, margin-top: 2px
Verify it matches the visual pattern of adjacent rules

quality_checklist.description: Health field naming is consistent across all locations
steps: Grep for 'maxHealth' and 'health' in game.js — verify naming matches the movesLeft/movesMax pattern
Verify HTML span IDs (health-current, health-max) are referenced correctly in JS

quality_checklist.description: No dead code or unnecessary additions
steps: Verify no extra functions, event listeners, or unused variables were added
Confirm no combat/damage logic was introduced
Check that only the minimum required lines were added
Verify var vs const usage matches existing patterns
Check indentation matches surrounding code (4-space base, 8-space inside functions)
Confirm CSS rule follows the same structure as #unit-moves and #unit-lifespan

checks.description: Unit info panel displays health for Elon Musk on page load
steps: Open http://localhost:8080 in a browser or fetch the page
Verify the #unit-info panel contains a #unit-health element
Verify it shows 'Health: 100 / 100' for the initially selected Elon unit

checks.description: Health updates when switching to a Rocktimus unit
steps: Build a Rocktimus Robot in-game (requires 2 energy + 1 rock)
Press Tab to cycle to the Rocktimus unit
Verify the health display changes to 'Health: 50 / 50'

checks.description: HTML page loads without JavaScript errors
steps: Open browser dev tools console
Load http://localhost:8080
Verify no JavaScript errors appear in the console
